commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.7.15: full bleed — the specimen plate floods past its own trim marks until the ink owns the sheet (#38)

Recast the takeover node. bmf rejected concept and craft ("mediocre idea, poor
execution... needs a revamp"), so this is a full recast, not a polish-in-place.

The new concept is earned from the gallery's own print-shop vocabulary: every
form-node wears a shared crop-mark specimen frame whose marks register "just
inside the trim." Takeover has an exact print meaning — full bleed: ink that runs
PAST the trim, edge to edge, no margin. So the finale is the one form that breaks
the frame its siblings share. A contained ember specimen plate floods outward; the
crop marks and catalogue label (the furniture that lives in the margin) are buried
by occlusion as the ink crosses; at full bleed only the headline and the
term-naming caption survive.

- ONE scalar (--bleed) drives the ink panel inset from a paper margin to past the
  sheet edge AND the live BLEED% readout, so figure and picture can never disagree
  [LAW:one-source-of-truth]. The margin furniture vanishes by geometry, not a
  second computed channel.
- Inks with var(--accent) (ember), fixing the old painter's hsl(250->330) lerp that
  ignored node.accent entirely. Matte ink, no bloom — the press lays ink, not light.
- The field takes over, not the word: the headline holds its size; the motion is
  the ink crossing the trim, the thing the form actually is (the rejected execution
  scaled the word and swelled a hue).
- smoothstep, complete at 0.9: the plate reads as a contained specimen before the
  flood starts, then settles into a held full bleed for the reading tail.
- Pure scroll-scrub from one scalar; no WAAPI added, so reduced-motion is untouched.
- Fixed the read-beat SCOPE comment that still named "takeover's swelling word".

Verified in Chrome at 810px across the full node range: --bleed scrubs 16%->-8%
monotonically, readout tracks 0->100%, zero console errors/warnings.
